entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The crop species studied for its ability to emerge through soil crusts

entities.name: SEEDLING EMERGENCE
entities.description: The ability of a seedling to break through the soil surface and emerge

entities.name: SOIL CRUSTING
entities.description: The formation of a hard surface layer on the soil that can impede seedling emergence

entities.name: ARIDOSOLS
entities.description: A specific soil type studied at Hisar that is susceptible to surface crusting

entities.name: HISAR
entities.description: The geographical location where Aridosols were studied

entities.name: ALFISOLS
entities.description: A specific soil type studied at Patancheru that is susceptible to surface crusting and soil hardening

entities.name: PATANCHERU
entities.description: The geographical location where Alfisols were studied

entities.name: SOIL HARDENING
entities.description: The process of soil becoming hard upon drying, observed in Alfisols

entities.name: CRUST STRENGTH
entities.description: The physical strength of the soil crust, which increases as moisture decreases and temperature increases

entities.name: SOIL MOISTURE
entities.description: The water content in the soil seed zone, which decreases as crust strength increases

entities.name: SOIL TEMPERATURE
entities.description: The temperature of the soil in the seed zone, which increases as crust strength increases

entities.name: GENETIC VARIABILITY
entities.description: The genetic differences among sorghum lines that affect their ability to emerge through soil crusts
